short_description: Situated in the Pacific Ocean some 1,000 km from the South American continent, these islands were the source of Darwin's theory of evolution.
Yellowstone National Park, the first national park in the world, was created in 1872 and covers 2 million hectares.
A globally significant coral reef ecosystem.
Tikal is one of the major sites of Mayan civilization, inhabited from the 6th century BC to the 10th century AD.
The Grand Canyon is a natural landmark carved by the Colorado River in Arizona, USA.
The gardens of Versailles, seat of the French royal court from 1682 to 1789, were a major influence on the development of art, architecture and garden design in Europe.

site: Memphis and its Necropolis – the Pyramid Fields from Giza to Dahshur
site_fr: Memphis et sa nécropole – les zones des pyramides de Guizeh à Dahchour
short_description: The ancient capital of the Old Kingdom of Egypt, Memphis, is one of the world's greatest cities of antiquity.

site: Historic Centre of Rome, the Properties of the Holy See in that City Enjoying Extraterritorial Rights and San Paolo Fuori le Mura
site_fr: Centre historique de Rome, les biens du Saint-Siège dans cette ville bénéficiant des droits d'extra-territorialité et Saint-Paul-hors-les-Murs
short_description: Founded according to legend by Romulus and Remus in 753 BC, Rome was first the capital of the Roman Republic and then the Roman Empire.
An intellectual and spiritual capital and a centre for the propagation of Islam throughout Africa, Timbuktu has a rich history.
The park's most notable feature is Mount Everest (8,848 m), the highest point on earth.
The Serengeti National Park in Tanzania hosts the greatest wildlife spectacle on Earth: the annual migration of wildebeest.
The Ngorongoro Conservation Area encompasses one of the world's most spectacular natural phenomena: a huge and virtually intact volcanic caldera.
An immense mausoleum of white marble, built in Agra between 1631 and 1648 by order of the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan.
Machu Picchu is the most spectacular urban creation of the Inca Empire, built in the fifteenth century.
The Iguazu Falls are one of the world's largest and most impressive waterfalls, situated on the border between Argentina and Brazil.
Petra is a large-scale prehistoric site in Jordan established possibly as early as the fourth century BC as the capital city of the Nabataean Kingdom.
Kaziranga National Park in Assam, north-east India, harbours two-thirds of the world's population of the great one-horned rhinoceroses.
Istanbul's strategic position on the crossroads of two continents has profoundly influenced the history of the city as a political and cultural centre.
Stonehenge and Avebury are among the most famous groups of megalithic monuments in the world.

site_fr: Parc national de la forêt impénétrable de Bwindi
short_description: This park is one of the last remaining habitats for mountain gorillas.
The Acropolis is the most striking and complete example of the ancient Greek civilization remaining today.
Tongariro National Park, New Zealand's first national park, contains outstanding examples of active and extinct volcanoes.
In the 3rd century BC and again in the early 15th century, the Chinese constructed the Great Wall as a military defense and border control system.
Brazil's capital, created in the 1950s by urban planner Lúcio Costa and architect Oscar Niemeyer, is a landmark in urban planning.
In a region of exceptional natural beauty, the six surviving monasteries of Meteora are built on natural sandstone rock pillars.
Chichen-Itza was the most important city of the Maya civilization in the Yucatán, flourishing from the 9th to 13th centuries.
Situated at the south-western tip of Java, this national park includes the Krakatoa Nature Reserve and is the last refuge of the Javan rhinoceros.
The Komodo National Park includes three major islands and supports the world's largest lizard, the Komodo dragon.
The Victoria Falls, known as Mosi-oa-Tunya ('The Smoke that Thunders'), is one of the world's largest and most spectacular waterfalls.
Himeji-jo is the finest surviving example of early 17th-century Japanese castle architecture, comprising 83 buildings with highly developed defensive systems.
Angkor is one of the most important archaeological sites in South-East Asia, the largest pre-industrial urban complex in the world.
The Sundarbans contains the world's largest area of mangrove forests, and is an outstanding example of ongoing ecological processes.
Luang Prabang is an outstanding example of the fusion of traditional Lao architecture with European colonial architecture.
Doñana National Park, in Andalusia in southern Spain, is one of the most important wetlands and wildlife reserves in Europe.

site: Tiwanaku: Spiritual and Political Centre of the Tiwanaku Culture
site_fr: Tiwanaku: centre spirituel et politique de la culture Tiwanaku
short_description: Tiwanaku is recognized as one of the most important precursors of the Inca Empire, flourishing as a major political center from approximately 500-900 CE.
With its snow-capped peak rising above 5,895 m above sea level, Mount Kilimanjaro is Africa's highest peak and one of the world's largest volcanoes.
The Danube Delta, in Romania, is one of the best-preserved deltas in Europe and provides critical habitat for many bird species.
The Pantanal is the world's largest tropical wetland area, covering 150,000 sq km in south-western Brazil.
Robben Island, in Cape Town Bay, was used as a prison for political prisoners during the apartheid era, most famously for Nelson Mandela.
The Western Ghats, also known as Sahyadri, is a mountain range that runs along the western edge of the Indian Peninsula and is older than the Himalayas.
The historic city of Gyeongju contains a remarkable concentration of outstanding examples of Korean Buddhist art and architecture.
Yin Xu is an ancient capital of the late Shang Dynasty in China, the first time that such a site has been confirmed through archaeological excavation.

site: Cultural Landscape of Bali Province: the Subak System as a Manifestation of the Tri Hita Karana Philosophy
site_fr: Paysage culturel de la province de Bali : le système des subak, expression de la philosophie Tri Hita Karana
short_description: The landscape of Bali consists of five rice terraces and their water temples that have been managing the ecology of rice terraces since the 9th century.

site: Prehistoric Rock Art Sites in the Côa Valley and Siega Verde
site_fr: Sites d'art rupestre préhistorique de la vallée du Côa et de Siega Verde
short_description: The Côa Valley in Portugal and Siega Verde in Spain contain the most important concentration of Palaeolithic open-air rock art in the world.

site: Nan Madol: Ceremonial Centre of Eastern Micronesia
site_fr: Nan Madol : centre cérémoniel de la Micronésie orientale
short_description: Nan Madol is an archaeological site lying off the eastern shore of the island of Pohnpei and represents a unique monumental island complex.

site: The Architectural Work of Le Corbusier, an Outstanding Contribution to the Modern Movement
site_fr: L'Œuvre architecturale de Le Corbusier, une contribution exceptionnelle au Mouvement moderne
short_description: Seventeen sites situated in seven countries across Europe, Asia and the Americas make up this serial property representing the work of Le Corbusier.
The serial property comprises the remains of three brick-walled and moated capitals of the Pyu Kingdom, inhabited between 200 BC and AD 900.
Ahmadabad, the walled city of the Gujarat Sultanate, was founded in the 15th century and is the first city in India to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List.
The forest complex in western Thailand at the Thailand-Myanmar border is a refuge for globally threatened large mammals.

site: Paraty and Ilha Grande – Culture and Biodiversity
site_fr: Paraty et Ilha Grande – Culture et biodiversité
short_description: This mixed site on Brazil's Atlantic coast presents an exceptional example of human settlement in a tropical coastal landscape.
